archimate:BusinessObject.name: Privacy Policy
archimate:BusinessObject.documentation:
Definition: Privacy Policy ABB is a Business Object that regulates the personal data of any customer, client or employee inforamtion.
Source: EU Data Protection
Source reference: https://commission.europa.eu/law/law-topic/data-protection_en
Example: The following implementation is an example of how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data - GDPR. https://eira-si.eu/privacy-policy/
Interoperability Dimension: Governance IoP
LOST view: Semantic view
Viewpoint: Interoperability Governance viewpoint
Interoperability Saliency: The Privacy Policy ABB is salient for semantic interoperability because it provides a guiding framework to ensure the privacy of data and information according to interoperability principles as stated in the EIF recommendation n° 46 and 47: "Consider the specific security and privacy requirements and identify measures for the provision of each public service according to risk management plans."
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/PrivacyPolicyBusinessObject
ABB name: eira:PrivacyPolicyBusinessObject
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2024-01-28
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/PrivacyPolicyBusinessObject
Interoperability Layer: Semantic

properties.value: Privacy Policy ABB is a Business Object that regulates the personal data of any customer, client or employee inforamtion.

The following implementation is an example of how this specific Architecture Building Block (ABB) can be instantiated as a Solution Building Block (SBB): Protection of individuals with regard to the processing of personal data - GDPR. https://eira-si.eu/privacy-policy/

The Privacy Policy ABB is salient for semantic interoperability because it provides a guiding framework to ensure the privacy of data and information according to interoperability principles as stated in the EIF recommendation n° 46 and 47: "Consider the specific security and privacy requirements and identify measures for the provision of each public service according to risk management plans."
